name: rioxarray (Rasterio + xarray Integration)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache-2.0); Corteva Agriscience / Alan Snow

performance.notes: Rasterio-backed; lazy loading via Dask; I/O-bound for large rasters

agent_readiness.notes: xarray accessor for geospatial raster operations. import rioxarray. ds = xr.open_dataset('dem.tif', engine='rasterio'). ds.rio.crs. ds.rio.reproject('EPSG:4326'). ds.rio.clip(geometries, crs). ds.rio.to_raster('output.tif'). ds.rio.write_crs('EPSG:4326'). ds.rio.set_spatial_dims(x_dim='x', y_dim='y'). ds.rio.bounds(). Resample: ds.rio.reproject(). Clip by geometry. Merge rasters. Integrates with geopandas, shapely. Cloud: COG, STAC via stackstac.

gotchas: Must import rioxarray before accessing .rio accessor
CRS must be set before most operations — silent failures without it
Clip by geometry requires geometries in same CRS as raster
Large rasters need chunk= parameter to avoid OOM (use Dask)
best_when: Reprojecting GeoTIFFs and NetCDF rasters in xarray workflows
Clipping rasters by vector geometries from GeoPandas
Writing CRS-aware rasters back to GeoTIFF
Combining rasterio and xarray for geospatial pipelines
avoid_when: When rasterio alone suffices (rioxarray adds xarray overhead)
Simple file format conversion without xarray needed
summary: rioxarray extends xarray with geospatial raster capabilities via rasterio. ds.rio.reproject('EPSG:4326'). ds.rio.clip(geometries). ds.rio.to_raster('output.tif'). ds.rio.crs. CRS management. Clip by vector. Apache-2.0 by Corteva. Best for xarray-based geospatial workflows combining rasterio reprojection/clipping with xarray analysis. Import rioxarray first to activate .rio accessor.
